projectDescription: MidnightBSD is a Unix-like open source operating system derived from FreeBSD, featuring a complete kernel, userland utilities, system libraries, and build infrastructure for multiple hardware architectures.
keyFeatures: Monolithic Unix-like kernel with modular driver support (KLD modules)
Multi-architecture support: amd64, arm64, i386, riscv, powerpc
Complete BSD-licensed userland with hundreds of system utilities
Rich system libraries: libc, libpthread, libcrypto, libssl, libpam, and more
Advanced virtual memory system with swap and memory-mapped files
Comprehensive networking stack: IPv4/IPv6, IPsec, firewall (ipfw/pf), wireless (net80211)
Multiple filesystem support: UFS/FFS, ZFS (OpenZFS), NFS, FUSE, cd9660, msdosfs
GEOM storage framework for modular disk I/O transformations
Jails for lightweight OS-level virtualization and containerization
Mandatory Access Control (MAC) framework for fine-grained security policies
DTrace dynamic tracing framework for kernel and userland
Capsicum capability-based security for privilege separation
mport package manager for binary package management
publicApiSurface: make buildworld / make installworld — Build and install the complete userland
make buildkernel / make installkernel — Build and install the kernel and modules
make universe — Build everything for all supported architectures
sysctl(8) — Read and write kernel state variables at runtime
kldload(8) / kldunload(8) — Dynamically load and unload kernel modules
pfctl(8) — Control the pf packet filter
ipfw(8) — IP firewall and traffic shaper control
jexec(8) / jail(8) — Create and manage BSD jails
zfs(8) / zpool(8) — Manage ZFS filesystems and storage pools
dtrace(1) — Dynamic tracing for performance analysis and debugging
libkvm — Kernel virtual memory access library
libprocstat — Process and file descriptor inspection library
libgeom — GEOM storage framework control library
